target_material: Uniform silver nanospheres
route_reported: Polyol synthesis in PEG followed by autoclave heating
phase_or_structure: Uniform face-centered cubic silver nanospheres
full_synthesis_procedure: PVP (444 mg, MW 40,000) was added to 40 mL PEG 600 under stirring at 80 °C. Once transparent, 1 mL of 0.5 M AgNO3 aqueous solution was rapidly injected. After the solution changed from colorless to light yellow during stirring and heating, it was transferred to an 80 mL Teflon-sealed autoclave and heated at 260 °C for 24 h. After air cooling, the particles were centrifuged at 16,000 rpm for 20 min and repeatedly redispersed and centrifuged in water or ethanol to remove PEG and PVP. The final sample was dispersed in ethanol.
notes: The optimal reported PVP/AgNO3 molar ratio was 8.

mixing_or_addition_sequence: Add PVP to PEG 600 and stir at 80 °C until transparent, then rapidly inject aqueous AgNO3. Continue magnetic stirring/heating until the solution changes from colorless to light yellow, then transfer to a Teflon-sealed autoclave.
washing: Centrifuge at 16,000 rpm for 20 min; redisperse and re-centrifuge several times in deionized water or ethanol.
post_treatment: Cool in air to room temperature and finally disperse purified nanospheres in ethanol.
yield: Reported as high yield, without a numerical value.

target_material: Fe3O4–Ag hybrid submicrospheres
morphology_reported: Hybrid submicrospheres decorated with Ag nanoparticles
route_reported: Two-step solvothermal synthesis and electrostatic assembly
phase_or_structure: Magnetite submicrosphere core with surface-assembled Ag nanoparticles
full_synthesis_procedure: FeCl3·6H2O (0.625 g, 2.5 mmol) was dissolved in 20 mL ethylene glycol, followed by 1.8 g sodium acetate and 0.5 g PEG. After 30 min vigorous stirring, the mixture was sealed in a 30 mL Teflon-lined autoclave and heated at 200 °C for 8 h. The black Fe3O4 product was washed and redispersed in 10 mL water. One millilitre of this dispersion was magnetically separated and redispersed in 9 mL trichloromethane plus 1 mL ethanol, mixed with 200 µL APTES, and sonicated for 8 h. The functionalized Fe3O4 was magnetically separated, washed, and redispersed in 10 mL water. Separately, 20 mL of 1 mM AgNO3 was mixed with 1.6 mL of 38.8 mM sodium citrate, followed by dropwise addition of 0.4 mL of 112 mM NaBH4 under vigorous stirring. The Ag colloid was aged for 24 h. Finally, 1 mL APTES-functionalized Fe3O4 dispersion was mixed with 22 mL Ag colloid and sonicated for 30 min. The hybrid spheres were magnetically separated, washed several times with water, and redispersed in 1 mL water.
notes: This is a composite synthesis, not a free-standing Ag powder route.

target_material: Ag nanospheres supported on ZnO nanorods
morphology_reported: Ag nanospheres supported on ZnO nanorods
phase_or_structure: Ag nanoparticles deposited on wurtzite ZnO nanorods
full_synthesis_procedure: Zn(NO3)2 (5 g) was dissolved in 100 mL ethanol and 1 M NaOH was added dropwise under stirring until pH 10. The mixture was irradiated in an open-vessel microwave reactor at one-third of 800 W for 10–15 min and removed at the onset of boiling. After cooling, ZnO nanorods were filtered and washed with water and ethanol. For Ag deposition, 4 g ZnO was dispersed in 100 mL aqueous 2 wt.% glucose and stirred for 10 min. AgNO3 solution was added dropwise until pH 7, then the mixture was microwave irradiated for 10 min and removed before boiling. After cooling, the Ag/ZnO product was centrifuged at 5000 rpm for 5 min and washed thoroughly with water and ethanol.
notes: The exact AgNO3 concentration/volume is not reported in the accessible text; cost calculations for the composite require the stated 1 wt.% Ag loading.

target_material: Seed-grown silver nanoparticles for a plasmonic DSSC monolayer
phase_or_structure: Colloidal Ag nanoparticles
full_synthesis_procedure: AgNO3 solution (0.30 mL, 10 mM) was injected into 20 mL of 1 mM trisodium citrate and vigorously stirred for 5 min. NaBH4 solution (1.8 mL, 10 mM) was added and stirring continued for 5 min. The seed solution was aged for 3 h at room temperature. For growth, 9 mL seed solution, 11 mL distilled water, and 1.2 mL of 20 mM sodium ascorbate were mixed, followed by rapid injection of 1.2 mL of 10 mM AgNO3 under vigorous stirring.
notes: Particle size was not explicitly reported in the accessible text.

target_material: Very-long, thin silver nanowires
phase_or_structure: Face-centered cubic silver nanowires
full_synthesis_procedure: Silver nitrate (15 mL, 0.02 M), D-glucose (0.12 g in 5 mL water), PVP (1 g in 5 mL water), and sodium chloride (15 mL, 0.04 M) were prepared separately. The PVP solution was prepared at 65 °C. Glucose solution was added to the silver nitrate solution with stirring. After 5–10 min, the PVP solution was added and the mixture was stirred for 20 min. Sodium chloride solution was then injected dropwise with continuous stirring. The resulting turbid hydrosol was transferred to a 50 mL Teflon-lined stainless-steel autoclave and heated at 160 °C for 22 h. After natural air cooling, the gray-white precipitate was centrifuged at 2500 rpm for 60 min, washed three times with distilled water and 3–4 times with isopropanol, and finally dispersed in isopropanol.
notes: The paper reports very-long Ag nanowires with lengths of 200–500 µm and diameters of 45–65 nm.

post_treatment: Air-cool autoclave naturally to room temperature; gently shake wires before centrifugation to avoid breakage and remove PVP; disperse final product in isopropanol.

full_synthesis_procedure: PVP (0.067 g) was dissolved in 10 mL glycerol and 3 mL ethylene glycol. In a separate vial, AgNO3 (0.034 g) was dissolved in 5 mL glycerol. target_material: Twin-crystalline silver nanowires used as templates for Pt nanotubes
phase_or_structure: Twin-crystalline Ag nanowires
full_synthesis_procedure: PVP (0.15 g, MW ~1,300,000) was completely dissolved in 30 mL of 0.02 M glucose solution under magnetic stirring. Fresh 0.05 M AgNO3 solution (0.8 mL) was added under continuous vigorous stirring. The mixture was transferred to a 40 mL Teflon-sealed stainless-steel autoclave and heated at 150 °C for 4 h. The resulting solution was sonicated for 10 min and used directly as a uniform Ag nanowire colloid.
notes: The paper's main product is Pt nanotubes; this record captures the Ag nanowire template synthesis.

target_material: Shape-controlled Ag nanoparticles and Ag nanoplates
route_reported: Polyol synthesis and aqueous PVP-mediated synthesis
phase_or_structure: Ag nanoparticles and two-dimensional Ag nanoplates
full_synthesis_procedure: For Ag nanoparticles, 3.0 mL of 94 mM AgNO3, 3.0 mL of 147 mM PVP (MW 55,000), and 6 mL polyol were mixed and heated at 140 °C for 1 h. Using 1,2-propanediol, 1,4-butanediol, or 1,5-pentanediol gave average sizes of about 60, 40, or 30 nm, respectively. Ethanol was added for centrifugation and collection. For Ag nanoplates, 100 mg PVP (MW 29,000) in 8 mL water was heated at 60 °C under stirring. AgNO3 (95.8 mg) was dissolved in 3 mL water and added to the PVP solution. The mixture was aged at 60 °C in air for 21 h, centrifuged, washed with ethanol, and redispersed in water.
notes: The Ceder morphology should be checked against whether the entry refers to the nanoparticle or nanoplate branch.

target_material: PVP-capped silver nanoparticles used as cores for AgNPs@MnO2
phase_or_structure: Uniform PVP-capped Ag nanoparticles
full_synthesis_procedure: PEG-600 (20 mL) was heated to 80 °C with magnetic stirring, followed by addition of 222 mg PVP. Once transparent, 0.5 mL of 0.5 M AgNO3 aqueous solution was added. The reaction mixture was transferred to a 50 mL solvothermal kettle and maintained at 220 °C for 24 h. After cooling, the PVP-capped Ag nanoparticles were centrifuged at 16,000 rpm for 15 min, redispersed in ethanol, rinsed several times, and finally dispersed in ethanol.
notes: For the elemental Ag route, use the first solvothermal step; downstream MnO2 coating is a separate composite-forming operation.

target_material: Closely packed silver nanoparticles grown directly on a silanized tapered optical fiber
phase_or_structure: Face-centered-cubic Ag nanoparticle layer on a tapered silica fiber
full_synthesis_procedure: AgNO3 (0.34 g) was dissolved in 30 mL deionized water and 1.5 g PVP was added under stirring. After 2 min, 0.269 g Na3C6H5O7·2H2O was added. The mixture was transferred to a 50 mL Teflon-lined autoclave and diluted with water to 80% of the autoclave volume. A 4.5 cm segment of silanized tapered fiber was immersed in the mixture. The sealed autoclave was maintained at 180 °C for 12 h and cooled to room temperature. The fiber probe was removed, rinsed with water, and vacuum-dried.
notes: This is a substrate-bound Ag synthesis rather than a free-powder route.

phase_or_structure: Face-centered-cubic Ag nanoparticle layer on a tapered silica fiber
full_synthesis_procedure: AgNO3 (0.34 g) was dissolved in 30 mL deionized water and 1.5 g PVP was added under stirring. After 2 min, 0.269 g Na3C6H5O7·2H2O was added. The mixture was transferred to a 50 mL Teflon-lined autoclave and diluted with water to 80% of the autoclave volume. A 4.5 cm segment of silanized tapered fiber was immersed in the mixture. The sealed autoclave was maintained at 180 °C for 12 h and cooled to room temperature. The fiber probe was removed, rinsed with water, and vacuum-dried.
notes: This is a substrate-bound Ag synthesis rather than a free-powder route.

pressure_or_vessel: 50 mL stainless-steel Teflon-lined autoclave filled to 80%
mixing_or_addition_sequence: Dissolve AgNO3 in water, add PVP under stirring, wait 2 min, add trisodium citrate, transfer to autoclave, immerse the silanized fiber taper, and hydrothermally treat.
washing: Rinse the fiber carefully with deionized water.
post_treatment: Vacuum dry after natural cooling.
support_or_substrate: APTMS-silanized tapered multimode silica optical fiber

target_material: Silver nanowires used to form PPy/Ag coaxial nanowire aero-sponges
phase_or_structure: Silver nanowires subsequently conformally coated with polypyrrole
full_synthesis_procedure: NaCl (6.9 mg) and PVP (2.64 g, MW 1,300,000) were added to 90 mL ethylene glycol at 90 °C. AgNO3 (1.02 g) was dissolved in 60 mL ethylene glycol and added dropwise to the first solution over 30 min. The mixed solution was heated at 160 °C for 6 h under solvothermal conditions. The Ag nanowires were purified by repeated centrifugation with water and ethanol.
notes: The paper focuses on the PPy/Ag hybrid, but the Ag nanowire precursor synthesis is fully described.

washing: Purify by repeated centrifugation with water and ethanol.
post_treatment: Used as an aqueous/ethanol dispersion for subsequent PPy coating.

target_material: Silver nanospheres used as sacrificial templates for hollow gold nanoshells
phase_or_structure: Colloidal Ag nanospheres
full_synthesis_procedure: AgNO3 solution (100 mL, 1.32 mM) was prepared. A separate 25 mL aqueous solution containing 0.0625 mg tannic acid and 0.09 g trisodium citrate was prepared. Both solutions were heated to 60 °C and combined under vigorous stirring. The mixture was held at 60 °C for 5 min, brought to boiling for 20 min, and then cooled.
notes: The main paper concerns gold reshaping; the Ag nanospheres are sacrificial templates.

additives: ["Tannic acid reducing agent", "Trisodium citrate reducing/stabilizing agent"]
mixing_or_addition_sequence: Prepare AgNO3 solution and a separate tannic acid/trisodium citrate solution. Heat both to 60 °C, combine under vigorous stirring, hold at 60 °C for 5 min, then boil for 20 min.
post_treatment: Allow the Ag colloid to cool; for subsequent gold-shell synthesis, centrifuge twice at 19,000 rpm for 10 min and redisperse in 0.1 M CTAB.

target_material: PVP-coated silver nanoparticles for conductive ink
phase_or_structure: PVP-coated Ag nanoparticles
full_synthesis_procedure: PVP (1.63 g), AgNO3 (2 g), and deionized water (100 g) were ultrasonically mixed at 25 °C. NaBH4 (0.4 g) was dissolved in 20 g water and slowly added dropwise under continuous magnetic stirring at 50 °C. After 1 h, the mixture was centrifuged at 10,000 rpm for 15 min, washed twice with ethanol, and redispersed in a water/ethanol/glycerol/ethylene glycol mixture (24:8:30:38 v/v) with 30 min ultrasonication.
notes: The paper later formulates a conductive ink and prints it on PET fabric.

post_treatment: Disperse in water/ethanol/glycerol/ethylene glycol (24:8:30:38 v/v) and ultrasonicate for 30 min.

target_material: Silver nanowires used as templates for Ag–AgCl/PPy nanocomposites
phase_or_structure: Ag nanowires subsequently partially converted to AgCl within PPy
full_synthesis_procedure: Three millilitres of 0.1 M AgNO3 in ethylene glycol and 3 mL of 0.6 M PVP in ethylene glycol were injected simultaneously into 5 mL ethylene glycol. The mixture was refluxed at 160 °C for 60 min. The Ag nanowires were centrifuged and washed several times with acetone.
notes: The elemental Ag route is the nanowire template synthesis.

washing: Centrifuge and wash several times with acetone.
post_treatment: Used directly as templates for CTAB-assisted pyrrole polymerization and FeCl3 etching.

target_material: Colloidal silver nanoparticles for plasmon-enhanced ZnO fluorescence
phase_or_structure: Crystalline Ag nanoparticles
full_synthesis_procedure: Ag nanoparticles were synthesized by an optimized colloidal reduction of AgNO3 using NaBH4, with PVP and trisodium citrate as stabilizers. The paper does not provide exact reagent quantities or reaction time in the accessible experimental text. The resulting pale-yellow, stable Ag colloid contained mostly spherical 20–40 nm particles.
notes: Exact precursor amounts are not reported and were not inferred.

target_material: BSA-stabilized silver nanoparticles
phase_or_structure: BSA-capped crystalline Ag nanoparticles
full_synthesis_procedure: A 30 mL aqueous solution of AgNO3 (0.03 M) was combined with BSA and stirred for 1 h. A reducing agent—24 mL of 0.4 M NaBH4, hydrazine, or DMF—was then added slowly. The mixture was stirred for about 12 h to complete formation of BSA-capped Ag nanoparticles.

target_material: Thiol-functionalized PCL-stabilized silver nanoparticles
phase_or_structure: Polymer-protected Ag nanoparticles
full_synthesis_procedure: One millilitre of 0.10 M tetra-n-octylammonium bromide in toluene was mixed with 1 mL of 0.05 M aqueous AgNO3 under rapid stirring. BPCLSH2 (0.042 g in 1 mL toluene) was added, followed by slow addition of 2.0 mL freshly prepared 0.25 M aqueous NaBH4. The reaction was stirred in the dark for 12 h. The organic phase was separated, concentrated by evaporation at room temperature, and dissolved in 20 mL chloroform.
notes: The paper also reports analogous linear-PCL-stabilized Ag nanoparticles.

pressure_or_vessel: Biphasic reaction vessel protected from light

post_treatment: Separate the organic phase, concentrate by room-temperature evaporation, and dissolve in 20 mL chloroform.
particle_size_nm: Approximately 2–3

target_material: Size-controlled silver nanoparticles for metal-enhanced upconversion luminescence
route_reported: Citrate reduction for 20 nm particles; polyol reduction for 100 nm particles
phase_or_structure: Crystalline Ag nanoparticles
full_synthesis_procedure: For ~20 nm Ag nanoparticles, 45 mg AgNO3 was dissolved in 200 mL water. A solution of 57 mg trisodium citrate dihydrate in 5 mL water was added under stirring, and the mixture was boiled for 15 min with vigorous stirring. After natural cooling, particles were centrifuged and washed with water several times. For ~100 nm particles, 1.25 g PVP was dissolved in 100 mL ethylene glycol, 0.25 g AgNO3 was added under stirring, and the mixture was heated at 130 °C for 15 min in an oil bath. After cooling, particles were centrifuged and washed with water.
notes: Both size-controlled routes are explicitly reported.

additives: ["Trisodium citrate for 20 nm route", "PVP for 100 nm route"]
solvent: Water for 20 nm particles; ethylene glycol for 100 nm particles
temperature_C: Boiling for 20 nm route; 130 °C for 100 nm route
mixing_or_addition_sequence: For 20 nm particles, add citrate solution to aqueous AgNO3 and boil under vigorous stirring. For 100 nm particles, dissolve PVP in ethylene glycol, add AgNO3 under stirring, and heat in an oil bath.
washing: Centrifuge and wash several times with deionized water.
post_treatment: Cool naturally to room temperature before isolation.
support_or_substrate: NaYF4:Yb/Er nanoplates in subsequent assembly experiments

target_material: Single-crystal silver nanoplatelets
phase_or_structure: Single-crystal face-centered-cubic Ag nanoplatelets exposing {111} basal facets
full_synthesis_procedure: A 20 mL DMF solution of 50 mM AgNO3 was added dropwise into 20 mL of 50 mM PVP in DMF. The mixture was transferred to a 50 mL autoclave and heated at 140 °C under autogenous pressure for 8 h. The product was isolated by centrifugation and washed with acetone and water.
notes: The paper reports single-crystal Ag nanoplatelets with {111} basal facets.

mixing_or_addition_sequence: Add the AgNO3/DMF solution dropwise into the PVP/DMF solution, then transfer to the autoclave.
washing: Centrifuge and wash with acetone and water.

target_material: Large silver nanoplates
phase_or_structure: Thin triangular or hexagonal Ag nanoplates
full_synthesis_procedure: AgNO3 (96 mg) and PVP (1.88 g, MW 29,000) were dissolved in 11 mL deionized water in a 20 mL vial at room temperature. After capping, the mixture was heated to 95 °C at 0.42 °C min−1 under magnetic stirring, held at 95 °C for 12 h, and cooled to room temperature.
notes: The heat-up method produces larger plates than the hot-injection comparison.

mixing_or_addition_sequence: Dissolve AgNO3 and PVP together in 11 mL deionized water at room temperature, cap the vial, and slowly heat to 95 °C at 0.42 °C min−1 under magnetic stirring.
post_treatment: Maintain at 95 °C for 12 h under magnetic stirring, then cool to room temperature.
particle_size_nm: Average lateral size ~1100

target_material: Shape-controlled silver nanoparticles
phase_or_structure: Single-crystalline face-centered cubic silver
full_synthesis_procedure: AgNO3 (3.214 mg) was dissolved in 172 mL deionized water and divided among four beakers (43 mL each). PVA solution (6.4 mL, 2 wt%), sodium citrate (6.4 mL, 30 mM), and H2O2 (0.2 mL, 30%) were added to each portion. After 10 min stirring at ambient temperature, 1.8, 2.5, 3.5, or 4.5 mL of 0.1 mM NaBH4 was added to the separate beakers and stirring continued until characteristic colors appeared. Products were centrifuged at 6000 rpm for 2 min, filtered, washed with deionized water, and vacuum-dried at 60 °C for 2 h.
notes: The mapping of NaBH4 volume to morphology is reconstructed from the reported order of solution colors and the paper's color-to-shape assignment.

post_treatment: Vacuum dry at 60 °C for 2 h.
particle_size_nm: Shape-dependent: spheres 10.6 ± 1.3; triangles edge 5.3 ± 0.4; rods length 11 ± 2.1 and thickness 4.8 ± 0.2; cubes edge 8.6 ± 0.4

PVA solution (6.4 mL, 2 wt%), sodium citrate (6.4 mL, 30 mM), and H2O2 (0.2 mL, 30%) were added to each portion. After 10 min stirring at ambient temperature, 1.8, 2.5, 3.5, or 4.5 mL of 0.1 mM NaBH4 was added to the separate beakers and stirring continued until characteristic colors appeared. Products were centrifuged at 6000 rpm for 2 min, filtered, washed with deionized water, and vacuum-dried at 60 °C for 2 h.
notes: The mapping of NaBH4 volume to morphology is reconstructed from the reported order of solution colors and the paper's color-to-shape assignment.

target_material: Silver nanoplates grown directly on a tapered optical-fiber tip
phase_or_structure: Silver nanoplates supported on tapered multimode silica fiber
full_synthesis_procedure: AgNO3 (0.4 g) was dissolved in 10 mL water. NaHCO3 solution (10 mL, 0.12 M) was added dropwise under stirring. After 2 min, PVP (2.5 g) was added and the mixture was stirred for 10 min. The mixture was transferred to a 50 mL Teflon-lined autoclave and the cleaned tapered fiber was immersed in it. The sealed autoclave was maintained at 170 °C for 5 h and cooled naturally. The fiber probe was removed, rinsed with deionized water, and vacuum-dried.
notes: This is a substrate-bound synthesis rather than a free-powder synthesis.

target_material: Highly dispersed spherical silver nanoparticles
phase_or_structure: Face-centered cubic silver nanoparticles
full_synthesis_procedure: AgNO3 (0.085 g) was dissolved in 10 mL ethylene glycol. PVP (0.1 g) was dissolved in 10 mL ethylene glycol at 60 °C under vigorous stirring. The PVP solution was added dropwise to the AgNO3 solution under stirring. The mixture was transferred to a 50 mL autoclave and held in a constant-temperature oven for 6 h. The oven was cooled naturally, the spherical Ag nanoparticles were centrifuged at 10,000 rpm, and the precipitate was redispersed in ethanol.
notes: The paper does not explicitly report the autoclave temperature in the accessible experimental text; it should remain null rather than be inferred.

washing: Centrifuge at 10,000 rpm to isolate nanoparticles.
post_treatment: Cool oven naturally to room temperature and redisperse precipitate in ethanol.

target_material: Silver nanoparticles and Ag/hydroxyapatite nanocomposite spheres
route_reported: Room-temperature chemical reduction followed by ultrasonic loading
phase_or_structure: Pure silver nanoparticles electrostatically loaded on hydroxyapatite nanoribbon spherulites
full_synthesis_procedure: Ethylenediamine (0.05 mL) was added to 20 mL of 0.10 M AgNO3 solution to adjust pH to 10. CTAB (0.01 g) was added, followed by 5 mL hydrazine hydrate solution dropwise over 5 min with stirring. The mixture was kept at room temperature for 5 min. Ag nanoparticles were centrifuged at 2500 rpm for 20 min, washed several times with deionized water and alcohol, and dried in a desiccator. For the composite, 0.6 g hydroxyapatite nanoribbon spherulites was added to 10 mL of a 2% Ag nanoparticle/alcohol dispersion, ultrasonicated for 2 h, and centrifuged at 1500 rpm for 10 min.
notes: For an elemental-Ag synthesis database, the first reduction step is the relevant route; the hydroxyapatite loading is a subsequent composite-forming step.

target_material: Silver nanowires and ordered silver nanowire arrays
route_reported: Hydrothermal nanowire synthesis followed by droplet-spreading alignment
phase_or_structure: Ag nanowires assembled into radially aligned arrays
full_synthesis_procedure: AgNO3 (0.17 g), PVP (0.2 g, MW 360,000), and sodium sulfide (7.2 mg) were dissolved in 20 mL ethylene glycol and vigorously stirred until clear. The solution was placed in a 50 mL Teflon-lined autoclave, heated at 160 °C for 5 h under autogenous pressure, and cooled to room temperature. The Ag nanowires were centrifuged and washed twice with anhydrous ethanol. For ordered arrays, an approximately 1 wt% ethanol suspension was dropped onto cleaned silicon or glass and allowed to spread and evaporate naturally at room temperature.
notes: The paper focuses on array ordering, but provides a complete hydrothermal synthesis for the Ag nanowires.

target_material: Silver nanostructures with controlled morphology
route_reported: Seedless solvothermal reduction with time-controlled reflux
phase_or_structure: Ag nanospheres, polygonal nanospheres, nanorods, and truncated triangular nanoplates
full_synthesis_procedure: AgNO3 (6.9 mg) and PVP (1 mg) were introduced into 5 mL DMF in a round-bottom flask and stirred at room temperature for 10 min, producing yellow small Ag nanospheres. The unwashed 5 mL colloid was then refluxed at 160 °C. One hour yielded polygonal nanospheres, 2 h yielded nanorods, and 4 h yielded truncated triangular nanostructures. Samples were cooled, centrifuged, washed with water five times to remove DMF and PVP, and redispersed in 3 mL deionized water.
notes: A single precursor formulation gives multiple morphologies through controlled reflux duration.

additives: ["PVP (capping agent); DMF also acts as reducing solvent"]
solvent: Dimethylformamide; products redispersed in deionized water
temperature_C: Room temperature for small nanospheres; 160 °C reflux for larger anisotropic structures
time_h: 10 min initial mixing; reflux 1, 2, or 4 h depending on target morphology
mixing_or_addition_sequence: Introduce AgNO3 and PVP into 5 mL DMF and stir at room temperature for 10 min to generate small Ag nanospheres. Reflux the unwashed colloid at 160 °C for controlled times to drive shape evolution.
washing: Centrifuge and wash with water five times; redisperse in 3 mL deionized water.
post_treatment: Cool samples to room temperature before repeated washing and redispersion.
particle_size_nm: Nanospheres 4–8; polygonal nanospheres 80–120; nanorods 70–75 × 30–38; truncated triangles side length 70–140
